title: Add a lap-group ranking specialist and blend it with the classifier
summary: Each race lap is effectively a small query set where only a few of ~20 drivers are plausible pit candidates.
rationale: Each race lap is effectively a small query set where only a few of ~20 drivers are plausible pit candidates. CatBoost ranking objectives are designed for within-group ordering, and QueryAUC directly measures whether positives are ranked above negatives inside a group. This matches the competition better than treating all rows as iid, and is a more concrete version of the earlier ranking idea.
implementation_hint: Create groups like (Year,Race,LapNumber) or (Year,Race,LapNumber,compound-regime). Train a CatBoost ranking model with `YetiRankPairwise`, `QueryCrossEntropy`, or optimize a standard loss while monitoring `QueryAUC`. Use the ranker only as an OOF meta-feature or low-weight blend with the strong binary booster; do not replace the classifier outright.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering among same-lap drivers, especially when multiple cars are near a pit window but only one or two actually stop.
risk: Ranking objectives can be slower and sometimes hurt global probability calibration; blending is safer than switching wholesale.
